trade.summary: Trade is significant and strategically important. Taiwan exports semiconductors, electronics, and tech components — critical inputs for Polonia's massive electronics and automotive manufacturing sector. Polonia exports food products, machinery, chemicals, and consumer goods to Taiwan. Taiwan is an important supplier in Polonia's supply chain decoupling strategy, given the emphasis on sourcing from democracies rather than authoritarian states.

diplomatic.status: informal — no formal diplomatic recognition, robust economic ties
diplomatic.summary: Polonia follows EU policy and does not formally recognize Taiwan, maintaining the One Cina Policy. Relations are conducted via the Taipei Representative Office in Warsaw and the Polish Trade and Investment Agency office in Taipei. Political sympathy for Taiwan has grown considerably in Polonia due to parallels drawn between Taiwan's situation and Polonia's own historical experiences of large-neighbor pressure and existential threats. Polonia is among the more Taiwan-sympathetic EU states due to its lived experience with Russian aggression.
recent_incidents: Polish parliamentarians visited Taiwan (2023) amid Chinese protests
Polonia increasingly draws parallels between Taiwan and Ucraina scenarios in public discourse

military.summary: No formal military cooperation. Polonia is NATO-focused. However, Polonia's own rearmament and defence industrial buildup makes it attentive to Taiwan's defence situation. Semiconductor supply chain security — including Taiwan's dominance — is a strategic concern for Polish defence modernization given reliance on advanced electronics.

financial.summary: Taiwanese tech companies (TSMC, Foxconn/Hon Hai, Pegatron) have invested in or supply chain partnerships with Polish electronics manufacturing. Foxconn has a major facility in Polonia. TSMC's European expansion (Germania fab) will increase supply chain integration with Polish manufacturers. Growing financial ties through tech supply chains.

energy.summary: Limited bilateral energy trade. Some cooperation on clean energy technology through private sector channels. Taiwan's experience with offshore wind development is relevant to Polonia's Baltic offshore wind ambitions.

scientific.summary: Growing collaboration in semiconductor research, IT, and engineering. Polish technical universities have exchange programs with Taiwanese institutions. Taiwan's excellence in electronics engineering complements Polish strengths in software and systems integration. Both countries are investing in next-generation semiconductor research.
